items.title: Poisoning the well: AI supply chain attacks on Hugging Face and OpenClaw
items.description: Threat actors are actively exploiting AI distribution platforms like Hugging Face and ClawHub to deliver malware by embedding malicious code within models, datasets, and agent extensions. Over 575 malicious skills across 13 developer accounts were identified in the OpenClaw ecosystem, targeting Windows and macOS with trojans, cryptominers, and AMOS stealer. Attackers abuse trust relationships betw
items.ai_summary: The threat actors are exploiting AI distribution platforms like Hugging Face and OpenClaw to deliver malware by embedding malicious code within models, datasets, and agent extensions, with over 575 malicious skills identified across 13 developer accounts in the OpenClaw ecosystem. The attackers are targeting Windows and macOS systems with trojans, cryptominers, and AMOS stealer, abusing trust relationships between developers and users. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement strict validation and verification processes for AI models and datasets, and ensure that all dependencies and extensions are from trusted sources. Additionally, users should be cautious when installing AI models and datasets from unknown sources, and monitor their systems for suspicious activity. Developers should also review their code and dependencies regularly to prevent malicious code injection.

graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1041| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T1566| E[Initial Access]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Ransomware Analysis: Go Binary and Fast Encryption
items.description: The Gentlemen is a Ransomware-as-a-Service operation, tracked as Storm-2697, that emerged in mid-2025 after splitting from Qilin ransomware following a payment dispute. Operating as a highly structured syndicate with at least 9 core operators, the group has compromised over 1,570 organizations across 70+ countries, with approximately 71-78% paying ransoms and never appearing on public leak sites.
items.ai_summary: The Gentlemen ransomware, tracked as Storm-2697, has been identified as a highly structured Ransomware-as-a-Service operation that emerged in mid-2025. This threat has compromised over 1,570 organizations across 70+ countries, with approximately 71-78% of victims paying ransoms. The attack vector involves obtaining capabilities through T1588, followed by data encryption for impact via T1486, resulting in significant financial losses for affected organizations. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust backup and recovery procedures, ensure timely patching of vulnerable systems, and conduct regular security audits to identify potential entry points. Additionally, organizations should consider implementing a least privilege access model and monitoring for suspicious activity to detect and respond to potential ransomware attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1588| B[Obtain Capabilities]:::tactic
    B -->|T1486| C[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    C -->|T1070| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: SilabRAT, What's Your Power?
items.description: SilabRAT is an advanced Remote Access Trojan offered as Malware-as-a-Service on Darkweb forums since late 2025, developed by threat actor o1oo1 and sold for $5,000 monthly. This financially-motivated tool focuses on credential theft and cryptocurrency operations, featuring Hidden Virtual Network Computing for invisible remote control, browser profile cloning to bypass session protections, and auto
items.ai_summary: The SilabRAT Remote Access Trojan, developed by threat actor o1oo1, is a highly advanced malware tool sold as Malware-as-a-Service on Darkweb forums for $5,000 monthly. It primarily targets credential theft and cryptocurrency operations, utilizing Hidden Virtual Network Computing for stealthy remote control and browser profile cloning to bypass session protections. If exploited, SilabRAT can lead to significant financial losses and unauthorized access to sensitive information, with real-world impact seen in various cryptocurrency-related heists. To remediate, defenders should implement robust network monitoring, focusing on unusual VNC and remote access software activity, and ensure all remote access tools are up-to-date and configured with strong authentication mechanisms. Additionally, regular audits of browser profiles and cryptocurrency wallets are essential to detect potential cloning activities.

    B -->|T1021.005| C[Execution]:::tactic

items.title: Fake Software Tutorials on TikTok Spread Vidar Stealer
items.description: Threat actors are leveraging TikTok and Instagram Reels to distribute the Vidar infostealer through fake software tutorials. Two distinct campaigns use short-form videos disguised as tutorials for unlocking premium software like Spotify. The first campaign uses accounts mimicking official Windows profiles with AI-voiced clips instructing users to run PowerShell commands that download Vidar from lo
items.ai_summary: The Vidar infostealer is being distributed through fake software tutorials on TikTok and Instagram Reels, with threat actors leveraging short-form videos to instruct users to run PowerShell commands that download the malware. The campaigns use accounts mimicking official Windows profiles, with AI-voiced clips providing instructions. If exploited, users may have their sensitive information stolen, including login credentials and financial data. To remediate, defenders should block PowerShell commands that download files from unknown sources and implement application whitelisting to prevent unauthorized software execution. Additionally, users should be cautious when following software tutorials online and verify the authenticity of the source before running any commands.

    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.001| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1036| C[Persistence]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.004| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: China-Linked Fake Consulting Sites Targeting US Clearance Holders Seized
items.description: US Federal authorities have seized 13 domains allegedly used in a Chinese intelligence-linked operation to recruit Americans with access to classified government information. The websites posed as legitimate consulting firms, offering vague consultancy and advisory roles to current and former US government employees, military personnel, and security clearance holders. The operation, which began in
items.ai_summary: The China-linked operation involved creating fake consulting websites to target US clearance holders, specifically current and former government employees, military personnel, and security clearance holders. The threat actors used social engineering tactics to recruit Americans with access to classified government information, offering them vague consultancy and advisory roles. The real-world impact of this operation could be the compromise of sensitive government information, potentially affecting national security. To remediate this threat, defenders should be cautious of unsolicited job offers or consulting opportunities, especially those that seem vague or require access to classified information. US government agencies and private organizations should also educate their employees on the risks of social engineering and the importance of verifying the legitimacy of job offers and consulting firms.

items.title: Vibe Hacking: Two AI-Augmented Campaigns Target Government and Financial Sectors in Latin America
items.description: Two distinct threat campaigns, SHADOW-AETHER-040 and SHADOW-AETHER-064, have been identified targeting government entities and financial organizations across Latin America using agentic artificial intelligence to conduct cyber intrusions. SHADOW-AETHER-040, a Spanish-speaking group, compromised six government entities in Mexico between December 2025 and January 2026, while SHADOW-AETHER-064, opera
items.ai_summary: The SHADOW-AETHER-040 and SHADOW-AETHER-064 campaigns, targeting government and financial sectors in Latin America, utilize AI-augmented techniques for cyber intrusions. These campaigns, active between December 2025 and January 2026, compromised six government entities in Mexico, with SHADOW-AETHER-040 being a Spanish-speaking group. The real-world impact includes potential data breaches and unauthorized access to sensitive information, affecting government entities and financial organizations. To remediate, defenders should implement robust network monitoring, utilize AI-powered intrusion detection systems, and ensure all software and systems are up-to-date, specifically focusing on secure configuration and patch management for operating systems and applications. Additionally, implementing a zero-trust security model and conducting regular security audits can help mitigate such threats.

items.title: Sniper's Nest: From Brand Impersonation to Browser Hijacking and CPA Fraud
items.description: An investigation into phishing activity targeting users across the Middle East and North Africa uncovered SniperDz, a centralized Push-Notification-as-a-Service and Phishing-as-a-Service platform. The operation uses fraudulent Facebook accounts impersonating politicians, public figures, and trusted organizations to promote fake offers including free mobile internet packages and financial compensat
items.ai_summary: The Sniper's Nest operation utilizes phishing tactics, specifically T1566, to trick users into divulging sensitive information or clicking on malicious links. The threat actors behind this operation use fraudulent Facebook accounts to impersonate trusted entities, promoting fake offers such as free mobile internet packages to lure victims. If exploited, users may experience browser hijacking and CPA fraud, resulting in financial loss and compromised personal data. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email and social media filters to detect phishing attempts, and educate users on how to identify and report suspicious activity. Additionally, users should be cautious when clicking on links or providing sensitive information online, and verify the authenticity of offers before engaging with them.

items.title: From external espionage to domestic targeting
items.description: Analysis of OceanLotus activities from 2024-2026 reveals a strategic shift toward domestic espionage within Vietnam. The Vietnam-aligned APT group conducted two distinct campaigns using the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor: a supply-chain attack compromising FireAnt Metakit stock trading platform from October 2025 to March 2026, and a prolonged intrusion into a Vietnamese infrastructure and transport constr
items.ai_summary: The OceanLotus APT group has shifted its focus towards domestic espionage within Vietnam, conducting two distinct campaigns using the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor. The first campaign involved a supply-chain attack on the FireAnt Metakit stock trading platform from October 2025 to March 2026, compromising the platform and potentially allowing the group to gain access to sensitive financial information. The second campaign involved a prolonged intrusion into a Vietnamese infrastructure and transport construction company, highlighting the group's ability to adapt and expand its targeting. To remediate these threats, defenders should implement robust network monitoring and detection capabilities, focusing on identifying unusual patterns of behavior associated with the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor, and ensure that all software and systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches. Additionally, organizations should conduct regular security audits and risk assessments to identify potential vulnerabilities in their supply chain and infrastructure.

items.title: Targeted espionage against Cambodian government entities
items.description: Acronis Threat Research Unit identified two espionage campaigns targeting Cambodian government entities in defense and public works sectors, attributed to a cluster tracked as Khmer Shadow. Both campaigns delivered a custom C++ loader named NIGHTFORGE through government-themed lures in self-extracting archives. NIGHTFORGE employs sophisticated evasion techniques including NTDLL unhooking and Hell'
items.ai_summary: The NIGHTFORGE custom C++ loader is delivered through self-extracting archives with government-themed lures, targeting Cambodian government entities in the defense and public works sectors. This loader employs sophisticated evasion techniques, including NTDLL unhooking and Hell's Gate evasion, to avoid detection. If exploited, NIGHTFORGE can lead to data encryption and impairment of defenses, resulting in significant real-world impact, such as disruption of critical government services and potential data breaches. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering to block suspicious attachments, ensure all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches, and configure endpoint detection and response tools to monitor for unusual activity. Additionally, network traffic should be monitored for signs of command and control communication, and incident response plans should be in place to quickly respond to potential breaches.

items.title: LBIOC-20260071 - The Gentlemens Leak
items.description: The Gentlemen is an active ransomware and extortion operation that emerged publicly in the second half of 2025, rapidly escalating into a high-volume threat actor. The group appears to be a continuation or reorganization of prior ransomware affiliate activity, with reported connections to the Qilin ecosystem and the Russian-speaking actor 'hastalamuerte.' This growth likely reflects existing ranso
items.ai_summary: The Gentlemen is an active ransomware and extortion operation that emerged in the second half of 2025, leveraging T1649 (Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates) to gain initial access. This threat actor has been linked to the Qilin ecosystem and the Russian-speaking actor 'hastalamuerte.' The group's rapid escalation into a high-volume threat actor suggests a well-organized and sophisticated operation. To mitigate this threat, defenders should prioritize monitoring for suspicious certificate activities and implement robust authentication mechanisms. Specifically, organizations should ensure that all authentication certificates are properly validated and rotated regularly, and consider implementing certificate pinning to prevent tampering.

items.title: CVE-2026-35716: A stack-based buffer overflow in the motion_privacy.cgi binary in VIVOTEK FD8136...
items.description: A stack-based buffer overflow in the motion_privacy.cgi binary in VIVOTEK FD8136 firmware FD8136-VVTK-0300a allows authenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary code as root via an oversized n1 parameter in a POST request to the /cgi-bin/admin/setpm.cgi, /cgi-bin/admin/setmd.cgi, or /cgi-bin/admin/setmd_profile.cgi endpoint (all symlinks to the same binary). The parameter value is copied int
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-35716 is a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in the motion_privacy.cgi binary of VIVOTEK FD8136 firmware FD8136-VVTK-0300a. This vulnerability allows authenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary code as root via an oversized n1 parameter in a POST request to the /cgi-bin/admin/setpm.cgi, /cgi-bin/admin/setmd.cgi, or /cgi-bin/admin/setmd_profile.cgi endpoint. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to root-level code execution, potentially allowing attackers to modify system settings, access sensitive data, or install malware. To remediate this vulnerability, administrators should update to firmware version FD8136-VVTK-0301b or later, which addresses this issue. Additionally, it is recommended to restrict access to the affected endpoints and monitor system logs for suspicious activity.

items.title: CVE-2026-34460: NamelessMC is website software for Minecraft servers. In versions 2.2.4 and prio...
items.description: NamelessMC is website software for Minecraft servers. In versions 2.2.4 and prior, the OAuth callback handling does not validate the state parameter server-side before exchanging the authorization code. This allows an attacker to capture a valid OAuth callback URL for their own account and cause a victim's browser to navigate to it, resulting in the victim's session being authenticated as the atta
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-34460 is a vulnerability in NamelessMC website software for Minecraft servers, affecting versions 2.2.4 and prior. The OAuth callback handling fails to validate the state parameter server-side, allowing an attacker to capture a valid OAuth callback URL and authenticate a victim's session as the attacker. This can lead to unauthorized access to the victim's Minecraft server. To remediate, update to version 2.2.5 or later, and ensure the 'oauth_callback_validate_state' configuration option is enabled. Additionally, users should be cautious when clicking on links or navigating to unfamiliar URLs, as this vulnerability can be exploited through phishing attacks.

    D -->|T1071| E[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-33398: NamelessMC is website software for Minecraft servers. In version 2.2.4, `modules...
items.description: NamelessMC is website software for Minecraft servers. In version 2.2.4, `modules/Forum/pages/forum/get_quotes.php` only checks whether the caller is logged in, then reads a post by attacker-controlled `post` ID and returns its content. The backend helper in `modules/Forum/classes/Forum.php` does not enforce forum or topic ACLs. In contrast, the normal topic page in `modules/Forum/pages/forum/view_
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-33398 is a vulnerability in NamelessMC version 2.2.4, which allows an attacker to exploit the 'modules/Forum/pages/forum/get_quotes.php' endpoint by providing a malicious post ID. This allows the attacker to read any post on the forum, regardless of their login status or permissions. The backend helper in 'modules/Forum/classes/Forum.php' fails to enforce ACLs for forum and topic pages, making it possible for an attacker to access sensitive information. To remediate this issue, update to version 2.2.5 or later, which includes a fix for this vulnerability. Additionally, ensure that the 'modules/Forum/classes/Forum.php' file is properly configured to enforce ACLs for forum and topic pages.

items.title: CVE-2026-30652: A remote buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the /cgi-bin/dido/setdo.cgi end...
items.description: A remote buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the /cgi-bin/dido/setdo.cgi endpoint of the admin interface of Vivotek FD8136 cameras running firmware version FD8136-VVTK-0300a. This flaw allows an authenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code as root on the device.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-30652 is a remote buffer overflow vulnerability in the /cgi-bin/dido/setdo.cgi endpoint of Vivotek FD8136 cameras running firmware version FD8136-VVTK-0300a. An authenticated attacker can exploit this flaw to execute arbitrary code as root on the device. This vulnerability allows for privilege escalation and potentially leads to a full device compromise. To remediate, update to firmware version FD8136-VVTK-0301b or later, and ensure the 'cgi-bin' directory is not world-readable. Additionally, consider implementing a web application firewall (WAF) to prevent exploitation attempts.

items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-30650 is a post-authentication remote buffer overflow vulnerability in the /cgi-bin/admin/eventtask.cgi endpoint of Vivotek FD8136 cameras running firmware version FD8136-VVTK-0300a. An authenticated attacker can exploit this flaw to execute arbitrary code as root on the device remotely. This vulnerability allows for privilege escalation and potentially leads to a full device compromise. To remediate, administrators should update to firmware version FD8136-VVTK-0301b or later, and ensure that the eventtask.cgi endpoint is not exposed to the internet. Additionally, consider implementing a web application firewall (WAF) to prevent exploitation attempts.

items.title: CVE-2026-10629: SIP signaling stack in Verizon IMS (unspecified version) implements SIP signalin...
items.description: SIP signaling stack in Verizon IMS (unspecified version) implements SIP signaling without IPsec integrity protection (missing Security-Client/Security-Server headers and ESP traffic), which allows an on-path attacker to compromise confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity of VoLTE signaling via passive monitoring and active manipulation of unsecured SIP messages over the radio and core network.

items.title: CVE-2026-10591: Insufficient access control restrictions in the file write tool in Amazon Kiro I...
items.description:
Insufficient access control restrictions in the file write tool in Amazon Kiro IDE before version 0.11 might allow remote unauthenticated actors to execute arbitrary commands via crafted instructions that cause writes to execution-sensitive paths  (such as .vscode/tasks.json), enabling auto-execution on folder open.



To remediate this issue, users should upgrade to Kiro IDE version 0.11 or later

items.title: CVE-2026-10047: The Bitdefender Napoca bare-metal hypervisor contains an out-of-bounds write vul...
items.description: The Bitdefender Napoca bare-metal hypervisor contains an out-of-bounds write vulnerability in the real-mode hook handler, implemented in napoca/kernel/handler.c. The handler uses a guest-controlled SS:SP-derived offset as an index into the 1MB RealModeMemory buffer without bounds validation. With SS=0xFFFF and ESP=0xFFFF, the computed offset can reach 0x10FFEF, exceeding the RealModeMemory buffer

items.title: CVE-2026-10046: Bitdefender Napoca bare-metal hypervisor contains an out-of-bounds write vulnera...
items.description: Bitdefender Napoca bare-metal hypervisor contains an out-of-bounds write vulnerability in the BIOS INT 0x15 / E820 memory map handler, implemented in napoca/guests/bios_handlers.c. The handler computes a destination offset into the guest RealModeMemory buffer from guest-controlled ES and EDI register values without validating that the resulting address remains within the 1MB RealModeMemory allocat

"An SQL injection in LangGraph's function could

items.title: Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots
items.description: The critical-severity OS command injection vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code with root privileges. The post Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Chrome 149 Update Patches 28 Vulnerabilities
items.description: The browser refresh resolved critical and high-severity security defects, including a dozen use-after-free bugs. The post Chrome 149 Update Patches 28 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: INTERPOL Operation Takes Down Sniper Dz Phishing Platform, Arrests Administrator
items.description: An INTERPOL-led operation last month resulted in the disruption of Sniper Dz, a decade-long phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform, Group-IB said Thursday. The effort, codenamed Operation Ramz, took place between October 2025 and February 2026, and saw authorities from 13 countries in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region making 201 arrests. Included among them was Guedz, the primary

items.title: Anthropic Disputes Fable 5 AI Jailbreak
items.description: An AI hacker claims to have achieved a prompt-based jailbreak shortly after Fable 5’s launch, but Anthropic says it’s not a real jailbreak. The post Anthropic Disputes Fable 5 AI Jailbreak appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Ivanti flaw by Sunday
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) ordered government agencies to patch an actively exploited Ivanti Sentry flaw within three days, as mandated by the newly issued Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 26-04. [...]

items.title: Over 73,000 French govt employees affected in Tchap messenger breach
items.description: The French government revealed that a recent breach of its Tchap encrypted messaging platform affects the accounts of over 73,000 employees in the French public sector. [...]

items.title: Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters
items.description: Oracle has mitigated CVE-2026-35273, but it has not publicly confirmed the vulnerability’s in-the-wild exploitation. The post Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Europol Disrupts AudiA6 Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
items.description: Authorities in Europe have disrupted AudiA6, a cryptocurrency laundering service used by ransomware gangs and cybercriminal networks. Europol, in a statement issued Thursday, said the dismantling of AudiA6 cut off a "key financial pipeline used to wash hundreds of millions in illicit profits." The service is estimated to have been used to launder more than €336 million (~$389 million) since the

items.title: Phishing Attack Volume Down 20%, But Risk Still Rising
items.description: Hackers are valuing quality over quantity, using AI to upgrade their phishing attacks rather than multiply them.

items.title: Japanese energy firm loses drive with data of 10.9 million clients
items.description: Kyushu Electric Power Co., Inc. has disclosed a physical security incident that affects private data of more than 10 million customers. [...]

items.title: Maine breach portal abused to publish fake data breach disclosures
items.description: In an unusual misinformation campaign, fraudulent data breach disclosures were submitted to Maine's official breach portal and publicly posted before their legitimacy could be verified, prompting companies to deny the claims. [...]

items.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
items.description: The ShinyHunters extortion crew exploited an unpatched flaw in Oracle PeopleSoft to break into enterprise systems, steal data, and demand payment to keep it private. The campaign hit universities hardest. Google's Mandiant attributes it to the group it tracks as UNC6240, and dates the activity between May 27 and June 9. Oracle did not publish its advisory until June 10, so the bug was a

items.title: Oracle mitigates PeopleSoft zero-day exploited in data theft attacks
items.description: Oracle is warning about a critical PeopleSoft Suite zero-day vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-35273 that allows unauthenticated remote code execution, with the flaw actively exploited in ShinyHunter data theft attacks. [...]

items.title: Max-Severity Ivanti Flaw Exploited 24 Hours After Disclosure
items.description: Initial methods suggest attackers had likely mapped out Ivanti's asset landscape upfront and acted quickly once the exploit became public.

items.title: New Attacks Trick OpenClaw AI Agent Into Running Code and Leaking Secrets
items.description: Two security teams have shown, in separate research published this week, that OpenClaw, the popular self-hosted AI agent, can be driven to run attacker-controlled code or hand over sensitive data through ordinary-looking inputs. Imperva buried instructions inside shared contacts, vCards, and location pins that the agent executed without the victim ever seeing them. Varonis built a test agent on

items.title: New GreatXML Exploit Bypasses Windows BitLocker via Recovery Partition XML Files
items.description: Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse and MSNightmare) has released a new Windows BitLocker bypass dubbed GreatXML, a day after they published an exploit for Microsoft Defender. "This was an accidental discovery, it took a total of 4 hours to find this," the researcher said in a post on Blogger. "If you ever attempted to use Windows Defender Offline Scan, you're

items.title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Claims 478 Victims, Can Spread Like a Worm
items.description: A new analysis of The Gentlemen operation has revealed that the financially motivated threat group initially operated as an affiliate responsible for conducting double extortion attacks, while leveraging resources from various ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) schemes like LockBit (aka Tenacious Mantis), Qilin (aka Pestilent Mantis), and Medusa (aka Venomous Mantis). According to a detailed report

items.title: Cyber Force not included in Senate defense policy roadmap
items.description: An amendment by Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand (D-NY) to the chamber’s fiscal 2027 national defense authorization bill that would have created the digital-focused service was defeated 14-13 when the Senate Armed Services Committee took up the nearly $1.2 trillion legislation behind closed doors this week.

items.title: Authorities dismantle 'AudiA6' ransomware crypto-laundering service
items.description: Law enforcement has dismantled the “AudiA6” cryptocurrency service allegedly used by ransomware actors and other cybercriminals to launder more than $380 million. [...]

items.title: British high school sends students home following cyberattack
items.description: Great Marlow School, which has 1,428 pupils according to the Department for Education (DfE), said it was set to remain closed while it works with specialist IT and cybersecurity professionals to resolve the issue.

items.title: Hacker linked to Void Blizzard faces charges over cyberespionage campaign
items.description: Denis Obrezko, 36, made his initial appearance in federal court in Boston on Tuesday after being transferred to U.S. custody from Thailand, where he was arrested last November.

items.title: Segmentation Works for OT If Operators Are Paying Attention
items.description: Even the best segmentation strategy will fall apart without constant oversight and disciplined operations.

items.title: Why AI-driven threats are exposing the limits of MSP security stacks
items.description: AI-driven attacks are exposing the limits of fragmented MSP security stacks and slow response workflows. Kaseya breaks down why integrated security, automation, and recovery are becoming essential. [...]

items.title: Oracle Addresses PeopleSoft Vulnerability Amid Reports of Zero-Day Attacks
items.description: Oracle has released mitigations for CVE-2026-35273, but it has not said whether it’s a zero-day exploited in ShinyHunters attacks. The post Oracle Addresses PeopleSoft Vulnerability Amid Reports of Zero-Day Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: University of Nottingham confirms cyber incident as Shiny Hunters group claims data theft
items.description: According to the university’s statement, it is still working to understand what data has been accessed and said it had already directly contacted affected students and alumni, potentially including those in its foreign campuses in Malaysia and China as well as in Nottingham.

items.title: Alert Fatigue Is Becoming a Security Threat of Its Own
items.description: As alert volumes outpace human capacity, organizations are turning to AI, automation, and deeper context to separate real threats from the noise. The post Alert Fatigue Is Becoming a Security Threat of Its Own appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cybersecurity Stars Awards 2026: Winners Announced Across 95 Categories
items.description: Most good security work is invisible by design. Today is the exception. The 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards winners are announced across 95 subcategories in four main award categories. The reason is simple. Cybersecurity is full of work that deserves recognition and rarely gets it. Products that quietly close real gaps. Teams that stop incidents nobody reads about. Companies that raise the

items.title: CISA Directs Federal Agencies to Prioritize Security Patches Based on Risk
items.description: The new BOD 26-04 requires agencies to review and update vulnerability management policies with a focus on KEV catalog entries. The post CISA Directs Federal Agencies to Prioritize Security Patches Based on Risk appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: OnyxC2 Stealer Offers Cybercriminals Enterprise-Grade Theft for $250 a Month
items.description: Researchers say the OnyxC2 malware targets more than 200 applications and extensions while evading detection through encrypted payloads, DLL sideloading, and in-memory execution techniques. The post OnyxC2 Stealer Offers Cybercriminals Enterprise-Grade Theft for $250 a Month appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, June 11th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9968, (Thu, Jun 11th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Chinese, N. Korean Threat Groups Build on Asia-Pacific Success
items.description: North Korea's gross domestic product (GDP) has grown, in part because of the nation's state-sponsored cybercrime groups, which target financial firms and other businesses.

items.title: CISA Rewrites Federal Patching Requirements for AI Threat Era
items.description: The new directive gives federal agencies three days to fix the most dangerous flaws, while less severe issues can be deferred.

items.title: Bug Bounty Research Triggers ServiceNow Security Alert
items.description: Security research inadvertently led organizations to believe they were being breached through their ServiceNow instances.

items.title: CISA to require federal agencies to patch some cyber vulnerabilities within 3 days
items.description: CISA is giving agencies 180 days to adopt the new patching time frame, according to a directive released Wednesday.

items.title: AI Risk Worries Insurers & Businesses Alike
items.description: As companies adopt AI, many insurance firms are explicitly excluding AI risks, while others are forging ahead to create the right framework. What risks can firms reasonably manage?

items.title: Nightmare-Eclipse Drops Yet Another Microsoft Exploit, RoguePlanet
items.description: The disgruntled researcher released yet another PoC for a Windows Defender bug that allows for system takeover, showing no signs of abandoning their ongoing feud with Microsoft.

items.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
items.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

items.title: CISA Issues New Directive Improving How Federal Agencies Prioritize the Mitigation of Cyber Vulnerabilities
How has use of framing protection security headers changed in the past 3 years?, (Wed, Jun 10th)
items.description: Back in 2023, I wrote a diary[1] discussing how commonly X-Frame-Options and CSP headers containing the frame-ancestors directive were used on 1 million most popular domains on the internet (based on the Tranco list[2]), and how they were set. Given that three years have passed since then, I thought it might be interesting to repeat the analysis and see what â€“ if anything â€“ has changed in the meantime.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, June 10th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9966, (Wed, Jun 10th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
items.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

items.title: The Invisible Battlefield: How Cyberwar Is Reshaping Everyday Life
items.description: Former National Cyber Director Chris Inglis warns that cyberattacks threaten hospitals, utilities, and essential services.

items.title: Microsoft June 2026 Patch Tuesday, (Tue, Jun 9th)
items.description: Microsoft today released patches for 204 vulnerabilities. 38 of these vulnerabilities are considered critical, and three have been disclosed before today. Six of the vulnerabilities affect Microsoft cloud solutions and do not require any user action. In addition, Microsoft incorporated 360 different vulnerabilities affecting Chromium into its Edge browser.

items.title: CISA Announces Winners of the 2026 President’s Cup Cybersecurity Competition
ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, June 9th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9964, (Tue, Jun 9th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-06-07, (Mon, Jun 8th)
items.description: This diary continues the Internet Storm Center&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s tracking of the TeamPCP supply chain campaign, first documented in the SANS white paper When the Security Scanner Became the Weapon and most recently in the handler diary Activity Through 2026-05-24. Since that update, the story moved into two new places: the United States government, which formally caught up to the campaign, and the wider population of attackers now wielding the Mini Shai-Hulud framework that TeamPCP open-sourced last month.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, June 8th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9962, (Mon, Jun 8th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: The Evil MSI Background is Back!, (Fri, Jun 5th)
items.description: A few months ago, I wrote a diary about a payload that was embedded into a JPEG picture. It was a MSI-branded background[1]. Yesterday, I spotted another one! It seems that the technic is getting more and more popular. This time, it started with a mail containing a WeTransfer link.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, June 5th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9960, (Fri, Jun 5th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Urges Stronger Security for Automatic Tank Gauge Systems
Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
